refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To ensure atomicity and clarity, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refined FR focuses solely on the load phase, avoiding any solution or implementation details and maintaining problem domain language.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, ensuring the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination
Adjust equipment operational parameters to meet cycle time
Implement real-time monitoring and adaptive control of load phase
Enhance operator training and procedural adherence for load phase efficiency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination

potential_sources: Interdependence between load phase and travel/dump phases if sequencing is not well isolated
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on load phase sequencing independently, minimizing coupling with other cycle phases by targeting process coordination within the load phase only.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time target is achievable through process optimization alone without hardware changes
Operational policies and safety plans do not impose constraints that prevent process sequencing changes
Real-time monitoring and adaptive control are considered secondary and not primary DP at this level
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive control mechanisms or real-time feedback as a DP if dynamic adjustment is critical to meeting cycle time targets under variable site conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing and optimizing operational processes including load phase sequencing and coordination

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges status and performance data relevant to load phase optimization

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends commands and coordination tasks to other modules or subsystems

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as load phase optimization is a core operational function; minimal diffusion as coordination commands and telemetry are well encapsulated.
Partial containment possible but load phase sequencing is more process-level than vehicle control; would require significant diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control rather than process optimization; containment weak and diffusion high.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not process sequencing; containment very weak.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' because it directly manages operational processes and can encapsulate the necessary coordination logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some sequencing requires vehicle state awareness.
Coordination commands may require careful interface design to avoid tight coupling with other modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase by focusing on process sequencing and coordination, which are key factors influencing cycle time. The DP is logically aligned with the FR and grounded in measurable performance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target).

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if some sequencing requires vehicle state awareness.
Coordination commands may require careful interface design to avoid tight coupling with other modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk, with a containment score of 8 and diffusion score of 2, indicating it is an appropriate and focused boundary for the DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data exchange and tasking commands that allow measurement of cycle time performance against the target under defined duty cycles and site conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The IM boundary is clearly defined as the NGHE Solution module responsible for managing and optimizing operational processes including load phase sequencing and coordination, with well-specified external interfaces (Telemetry and Tasking) supporting the DP realization.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface requirements with the Vehicle module to manage potential overlap and ensure necessary vehicle state information is accessible without increasing diffusion.
Define interface contracts for coordination commands to minimize coupling and maintain modularity.
Consider documenting how safety policies and worksite safety plans are integrated or referenced within the NGHE Solution to ensure compliance and holistic system behavior.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong alignment, feasible verification, and well-defined ownership boundaries. The NGHE Solution module is an appropriate implementation module with high cohesion and low diffusion risk. Recommended revisions pertain to interface clarifications and documentation rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable decomposition is prudent before proceeding further.

revise.why: Clarify interface requirements with the Vehicle module and define interface contracts for coordination commands to minimize coupling and maintain modularity, as well as document integration of safety policies within the NGHE Solution.

assumptions: The DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' sufficiently captures the functional requirement for cycle time target achievement.
The NGHE Solution module is the correct ownership boundary for the DP realization.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module requiring careful interface design to avoid tight coupling.
Ensuring safety policies and worksite safety plans are properly integrated or referenced within the NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating cycle time performance under defined duty cycles and site conditions.
Interface contracts and documentation showing minimized coupling and clear responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Safety compliance documentation related to the NGHE Solution module.
